title: Campylopora australiana
taxonomy: Campylopora australiana is classified as a species of lichenized fungus within the genus Campylopora. It belongs to the family Pilocarpaceae and the order Lecanorales. The classification is based on its morphological and molecular characteristics. This classification helps in understanding the evolutionary relationships and ecological roles of Campylopora australiana. The classification also aids in identifying and differentiating it from other similar species.
uses: Campylopora australiana is a plant species native to Australia, and research has shown potential for its use in traditional medicine.
